color.quote: Numerous tiny dark brown to black specks are scattered across the green leaflets, with patches of yellow chlorosis surrounding clusters of lesions, particularly on the central leaflet.

shape.quote: Lesions appear as very small, roughly circular pinpoint specks rather than large angular or irregular blotches.

margin.quote: Individual specks have indistinct edges and coalesce into chlorotic yellow zones, giving the affected areas a diffuse rather than sharply bordered appearance.

texture.quote: The lesions appear flat and dry against the leaf surface with no visible sporulation, ooze, or raised pustules.

color.quote: Numerous small lesions appear dark brown to black with subtle purplish-brown halos scattered across a dull green leaf surface.

shape.quote: Lesions are mostly small and roughly circular, with some coalescing into larger irregular blotches as they merge.

margin.quote: Spot edges are not sharply defined; each dark center fades into a faint purplish-brown halo against the surrounding leaf tissue.

texture.quote: Affected tissue looks dry and necrotic, with the leaf curled and torn where lesions have coalesced and the blade has split.

color.quote: The foliage shows uniform green to dark-green coloration with no obvious chlorotic mottle or yellowing visible in this studio shot.

shape.quote: Leaflets are dramatically reduced to narrow, strap-like, shoestring/fern-like blades on bare elongated stems, characteristic of CMV shoestring-type distortion.

margin.value: irregular, deeply lobed and puckered, reduced nearly to the midrib
margin.quote: Leaf margins are highly irregular, deeply incised and puckered, with the laminae reduced almost to the midrib in many leaflets.

texture.quote: Foliage is crinkled, rugose and twisted with stunted, malformed leaflets clustered along thin stems.

color.quote: From this wide field-distance shot the foliage reads as a uniform dull green with no resolvable mosaic mottling, yellowing, or chlorotic patterning visible on individual leaves.

shape.value: stunted, drooping plants with an uneven canopy
shape.quote: The staked tomato plants are conspicuously short and uneven in height with a limp, drooping habit, several rows failing to reach the tops of their wooden stakes.

margin.quote: Individual leaf margins are not resolvable at this field-scale viewing distance, so margin character cannot be determined from this photo.

texture.quote: Fine leaf-surface texture (puckering, rugosity, shoestring narrowing) cannot be discerned from this distant field overview.

color.quote: The diseased tomato foliage in this Alabama field is overwhelmingly dark brown to blackened across whole plants, with little remaining green tissue and ripening red/orange fruit still hanging on the dying vines.

texture.quote: The canopy looks dry, shriveled, and largely defoliated — leaves are crispy and collapsed onto the stakes rather than turgid, indicating advanced late-stage necrosis rather than early spotting.

color.quote: Lesions are dark brown to tan in the center with surrounding chlorotic yellowing of adjacent leaf tissue.

shape.value: roughly circular to irregular concentric-ringed spots
shape.quote: Spots are roughly circular to slightly irregular with visible concentric rings forming a target-like pattern.

margin.value: well-defined dark border with diffuse yellow halo
margin.quote: Each lesion has a sharply defined dark brown border surrounded by a diffuse chlorotic yellow halo blending into healthy tissue.

texture.quote: Lesions show characteristic concentric ring (target-spot) patterning with a dry, papery appearance and no visible sporulation or fuzz.

color.quote: A green tomato fruit shows a sunken central lesion with a dense white-to-gray fungal mass surrounded by tan, papery, bleached tissue against the otherwise pale-green fruit surface.

shape.value: roughly circular sunken lesion with a star-shaped radial cracking pattern
shape.quote: The lesion is a roughly round, sunken depression at the stem-end region with star-like radial splits extending outward from the central fungal colony.

margin.value: irregular, jagged, cracked border between papery tan necrotic tissue and the green fruit surface
margin.quote: The lesion margin is irregular and jagged, defined by cracked, peeling tan papery tissue that transitions abruptly to the firm green fruit skin.

texture.quote: The center shows a dense, felty white-gray mycelial cushion ringed by dry, papery, cracked necrotic tissue, with the overall lesion surface sunken below the surrounding firm fruit skin; no distinct dusty gray spore powder is clearly visible in this single image.

color.quote: The lesion is a uniform tan-to-light-brown / beige color, with a fully necrotic background leaflet that has dried to a darker straw-brown.

shape.value: V-shaped wedge extending inward from the leaflet tip
shape.quote: Necrosis forms a broad V-shaped wedge that begins at the distal tip of the leaflet and expands back toward the midrib, rather than appearing as discrete spots.

margin.value: fairly distinct and slightly irregular boundary between tan dead tissue and green healthy tissue, no obvious yellow halo or water-soaked zone
margin.quote: The boundary between the tan dead tissue and the surrounding green leaflet is fairly distinct and slightly irregular, without an obvious yellow halo or water-soaked zone.

texture.quote: The affected tissue looks dry, papery and slightly crinkled, with no fluffy gray sporulation clearly visible on the lesion surface in this single field photograph.

color.quote: The affected stem and wilted foliage show a bleached tan to light-brown coloration that contrasts sharply with the surrounding healthy green leaves.

shape.value: elongated stem lesion girdling the main stem with collapsed wilted foliage above
shape.quote: The lesion appears as an elongated, girdling discoloration along the main stem, with the entire upper portion of the plant collapsed and hanging in a wilted, desiccated mass.

margin.quote: The boundary between the bleached, dead tissue and adjacent green tissue is gradual and diffuse rather than sharply outlined.

texture.quote: The dead leaves and stem segments appear dry, papery, and shriveled, with no visible white mycelium or black sclerotia clearly evident in this single field view.

color.quote: A dense white cottony fungal growth sits at the stem junction transitioning into a bleached tan/straw-colored shredded stem section, contrasting with the still-green upper stem tissue.

shape.value: stem-girdling lesion at branch crotch with cottony mycelial mass
shape.quote: The lesion girdles the main stem at a branch junction, with a cushion-like cottony mycelial mass on one side and a collapsed, hollowed stem section extending outward.

margin.value: diffuse transition from green healthy tissue to bleached necrotic tissue
margin.quote: The boundary between green healthy stem and bleached tan diseased tissue is irregular and diffuse rather than sharply defined, with the white mycelium overlapping the transition zone.

texture.quote: The infected zone shows fluffy cotton-like white mycelium at the stem base and a dry, shredded, fibrous straw-colored texture where the stem has been hollowed and bleached.

color.quote: Affected leaf tissue shows large tan-to-brown necrotic patches with a slightly greyish cast, contrasting against the surrounding green foliage.

shape.value: large irregular blotches expanding from leaf tips and margins
shape.quote: Lesions are broad, irregular blotches that have expanded inward from the leaf tips and margins rather than forming small discrete spots.

margin.value: irregular, diffuse, pale water-soaked transition into green tissue
margin.quote: The boundary between dead tan tissue and healthy green tissue is irregular and somewhat diffuse, with a faint pale/water-soaked-looking transition zone but no sharp dark border.

texture.quote: The necrotic areas appear dry and papery with some curling and tearing of the dead tissue, and no obvious white sporulation fuzz is visible in this sunlit photo.

value: large greasy/oily-looking dark blotches on fruit surface
firm, leathery brown lesion with sunken cracked area
no visible white sporulation on these fruits
distinctive_signs.quote: Three green tomatoes show large greasy, dark olive-brown to grayish blotches covering significant portions of the fruit surface, with one fruit displaying a sunken, cracked tan area at the lesion margin.

plant_parts.quote: Only immature green tomato fruits are shown, with attached calyx/stem on two of them; no foliage is visible.

color.quote: Lesions appear as olive-brown to grayish-green discolored patches blending into darker chocolate-brown zones, with a tan/cream cracked area on the right fruit, against the otherwise pale yellow-green unripe fruit.

shape.value: Large, irregular, diffuse blotches with indistinct margins covering the upper shoulders and sides of the fruit, roughly oval to amorphous and somewhat sunken/leathery in appearance.
shape.quote: Lesions are large, irregularly shaped diffuse patches with poorly defined edges that cover much of the fruit shoulder and side, appearing slightly sunken and leathery.

color.quote: Lesions appear as small dark brown spots, many with paler tan or grayish-tan centers, scattered across green leaflets with some surrounding tissue showing faint yellowing and larger necrotic tan-brown patches near leaf margins.

shape.value: circular to subcircular small spots, with some coalescing into irregular necrotic patches
shape.quote: Most lesions are small, roughly circular pinpoint-to-pea-sized spots; where multiple spots have merged near the leaf edges they form larger irregular necrotic blotches.

margin.value: well-defined dark brown border, sometimes with a narrow chlorotic halo
margin.quote: Individual spots show a sharply defined darker brown rim around a slightly paler center, and edge lesions transition into brown necrotic tissue with a thin yellowed margin.

value: tiny dark specks (possible pycnidia) within centers
texture.quote: Lesion centers look dry and papery, and several spots contain very fine dark pinpoint specks consistent with pycnidia, while heavily infected leaflet margins are shriveled and crisped.

value: numerous small circular spots scattered across leaf blade
spots with darker brown/grey margins and paler tan centers
yellow chlorotic halos and yellowing along leaf margins
small necrotic perforation/hole where lesion tissue has dropped out
distinctive_signs.quote: The leaf shows many small circular spots with darker rims and paler centers, surrounded by yellowing tissue and one tear/hole where necrotic tissue has fallen out.

plant_parts.quote: A single detached tomato leaflet is shown upper-surface up against a red background, with lesions across the blade and along the margins.

value: greyish-brown to dark brown spot margins
yellow chlorotic halos and marginal yellowing
color.quote: Lesions are tan-grey at the center with darker brown borders and yellow halos, set against an otherwise green leaf with yellowing along the edges.

shape.value: small circular to roughly round spots, 1–3 mm in diameter, fairly uniform in size and densely scattered across the blade
shape.quote: The lesions are small, round, and uniform in size, peppered densely across the leaflet rather than coalescing into large blotches.

value: tan-brown vascular discoloration at cut end
color.quote: The stem itself is bright green while the freshly exposed cut surface shows a tan-to-brown ring of discolored vascular tissue.

value: bacterial ooze streaming from cut stem suspended in water
milky/cloudy threads and fine droplets visible in the water column
vascular browning at the cut end
distinctive_signs.quote: A cut tomato stem is suspended in a clear container of water and is releasing fine milky droplets and streams — the classic ooze/streaming diagnostic for Ralstonia solanacearum.

plant_parts.quote: Only a severed length of tomato stem is shown, with its cut basal end exposed and dangling into water; no leaves, fruit, or roots are visible.

shape.value: short cylindrical stem segment with a clean transverse cut, showing a roughly circular cross-section with a discolored vascular ring and threads of ooze trailing downward into the water
shape.quote: The diagnostic structure is the cylindrical cut-stem cross-section with a ring of browned vascular tissue and fine ooze threads trailing into the water below.

value: brown vascular discoloration extending continuously along the length of a longitudinally split stem
darkened/decayed pith tissue contrasting with intact green outer stem walls
discoloration continues through the branch junction into the side shoot
distinctive_signs.quote: A longitudinally split tomato stem reveals an unbroken brown internal discoloration of the vascular and pith tissues running the full length of the main stem and continuing into a lateral branch, while the thin outer green rind remains intact.

plant_parts.quote: The image shows a dissected tomato stem split lengthwise, exposing the internal vascular tissue and pith from the lower stem up through a node and into a lateral branch.

value: tan to medium brown (interior pith/vascular tissue)
darker chocolate-brown patches near the node
bright green (outer stem rind and edges)
color.quote: The split-open interior is a fairly uniform tan-to-medium-brown with slightly darker brown zones near the branch junction, framed by the bright green of the intact outer stem walls.

shape.value: Continuous, elongated longitudinal streak of internal browning following the central axis of the stem and branching with it at the node; no discrete spots or lesions on the outer surface.
shape.quote: The discoloration is a single continuous longitudinal streak filling the central pith along the entire stem axis and tracking into the side branch, rather than a localized spot or ring.

color.quote: White-to-cream cottony mycelium fans across a brown tissue/substrate surface that is dotted with numerous tan to light-brown, mustard-seed-colored sclerotia.

shape.value: small round/spherical sclerotia (mustard-seed-like) embedded in a fan-shaped, radially spreading mycelial mat
shape.quote: The image shows tiny uniformly spherical sclerotia scattered in concentrated patches over a fan-shaped, radiating mat of mycelium rather than a discrete leaf lesion.

margin.value: diffuse, feathery, indistinct mycelial edge with no sharp lesion border
margin.quote: The mycelial growth has a soft, feathery, indistinct edge that fades into the surrounding substrate without a defined lesion margin.

texture.quote: The surface combines a fluffy, cottony white mycelial mat with a granular, beaded layer of small spherical sclerotia clustered most heavily on the right side of the specimen.

color.quote: The three uprooted tomato plants show tan-to-brown desiccated foliage with patches of remaining yellow-green tissue and a few still-green immature fruits, while the exposed root mass is dark brown with adhering soil.

shape.quote: The image shows whole uprooted plants that have wilted and died as a unit rather than discrete localized lesions, consistent with girdling at the crown.

texture.quote: Foliage appears dried, shriveled and papery with curled leaflets; no white mycelial mats or mustard-seed sclerotia are resolvable at this wide-shot scale.

color.quote: The leaflets show a dull bronze-green cast with scattered small dark brown to purplish-brown flecks distributed across the upper surface.

shape.value: small irregular flecks and pinpoint spots with faint ring-like markings
shape.quote: Symptoms appear as numerous small irregular flecks and pinpoint spots, with a few faint concentric ring-like markings on the leaf surface.

margin.quote: The flecks have diffuse, poorly defined margins that blend gradually into the surrounding bronzed leaf tissue rather than forming sharp borders.

texture.quote: The leaf surface looks bronzed and slightly puckered/rugose between the veins, with no visible sporulation or fuzzy growth.

color.quote: Healthy tissue is medium green, with diffuse bronze-to-brown discoloration across the upper leaflets and darker brown-to-near-black necrotic patches concentrated on the smaller left leaf.

shape.quote: Symptoms appear as irregular, blotchy necrotic patches and diffuse bronzed areas rather than discrete circular spots or concentric ringspots.

margin.quote: Lesion edges fade gradually into surrounding green tissue with no sharp or haloed border between necrotic and healthy areas.

texture.quote: Leaflets are distorted with downward cupping and puckering, and the darker necrotic zones on the left leaf appear dry and papery while the bronzed areas remain flat against the leaf surface.
